title: General Tech Acronyms & Terms - 50 Words
definition: 50 common IT acronyms and general technology terms used across everyday workplace conversations.

words.meaning: Central Processing Unit - the main processor executing instructions in a computer.
words.sentence: The CPU is running hot after hours of heavy processing.

words.meaning: Graphical User Interface - a visual way of interacting with software.
words.sentence: The new GUI makes the app much easier to navigate.

words.meaning: User Experience - how a person feels when interacting with a product.
words.sentence: We hired a UX researcher to study how customers use the app.

words.meaning: User Interface - the visual elements users interact with in software.
words.sentence: The UI redesign made buttons more visible and consistent.

words.meaning: Software as a Service - software delivered over the internet by subscription.
words.sentence: We switched to a SaaS tool for our accounting needs.

words.meaning: Business-to-Business - transactions or services between companies.
words.sentence: This platform focuses on B2B sales rather than consumers.

words.meaning: Business-to-Consumer - transactions or services directed at individual customers.
words.sentence: Our B2C app lets customers order directly from their phones.

words.meaning: Key Performance Indicator - a measurable value showing progress toward a goal.
words.sentence: Our main KPI this quarter is customer retention.

words.meaning: Return on Investment - a measure of profitability relative to cost.
words.sentence: The marketing campaign delivered a strong ROI.

words.meaning: Service Level Agreement - a contract defining expected service standards.
words.sentence: Our SLA guarantees a response within four hours.

words.meaning: Frequently Asked Questions - a list of common questions and answers.
words.sentence: Check the FAQ page before contacting support.

words.meaning: User Acceptance Testing - testing done by end users before release.
words.sentence: We're waiting on client sign-off after UAT.

words.meaning: Minimum Viable Product - a basic version of a product used to test ideas.
words.sentence: We launched an MVP to validate demand before building more features.

words.meaning: Application Programming Interface - rules allowing software systems to communicate.
words.sentence: We integrated a third-party API for payments.

words.meaning: Uniform Resource Locator - the web address of a resource.
words.sentence: Click the URL to visit the documentation page.

words.meaning: HyperText Transfer Protocol - the protocol used to load webpages.
words.sentence: Most websites now use secure HTTP, known as HTTPS.

words.meaning: Operating System - software managing a computer's hardware and programs.
words.sentence: This app is compatible with both major operating systems.

words.meaning: Identifier - a unique code used to reference a specific record or user.
words.sentence: Each customer has a unique ID in our database.

words.meaning: For Your Information - used to share information without requiring action.
words.sentence: FYI, the meeting has been moved to 3 PM.

words.meaning: End of Day - by the conclusion of the current working day.
words.sentence: Please send the report by EOD.

words.meaning: As Soon As Possible - requesting urgent action.
words.sentence: We need this fixed ASAP before the client demo.

words.meaning: To Be Determined - not yet decided or scheduled.
words.sentence: The launch date is still TBD.

words.meaning: Not Applicable - indicating a field or question doesn't apply.
words.sentence: This section is N/A for our use case.

words.meaning: Too Long; Didn't Read - a brief summary of a longer piece of text.
words.sentence: TL;DR: the update fixes three bugs and adds dark mode.

words.meaning: Work From Home - working remotely instead of at an office.
words.sentence: I'm WFH today, but reachable on chat.

words.meaning: A small project demonstrating whether an idea is technically feasible.
words.sentence: We built a POC to test if this integration was even possible.

words.meaning: Request for Proposal - a document soliciting bids from vendors.
words.sentence: We sent an RFP to three vendors for the new system.

words.meaning: The use of computers and systems to manage and process information.
words.sentence: IT installed the new software on all company laptops.

words.meaning: The process ensuring software meets quality standards before release.
words.sentence: QA found two bugs before we shipped the update.

words.meaning: Activities aimed at innovation and developing new products or technologies.
words.sentence: Our R&D team is exploring new AI applications.

words.meaning: The executive responsible for a company's technology strategy.
words.sentence: The CTO approved the new cloud migration plan.

words.meaning: The executive responsible for managing information technology systems.
words.sentence: The CIO oversees all IT infrastructure decisions.

words.meaning: Software providing tools for writing, testing, and debugging code.
words.sentence: I write all my code in this IDE.

words.meaning: A text-based interface for interacting with software.
words.sentence: Some developers prefer the CLI over graphical tools.

words.meaning: A tool that converts long web addresses into shorter ones.
words.sentence: We used a URL shortener to make the link easier to share.

words.meaning: A person with deep knowledge in a specific area.
words.sentence: We consulted an SME before finalizing the security policy.

words.meaning: A formal evaluation of a proof-of-concept's results and feasibility.
words.sentence: The POC review confirmed we should proceed with full development.

words.meaning: End of Business - by the close of the business day.
words.sentence: Please confirm by EOB today.

words.meaning: Also Known As - used to indicate an alternative name.
words.sentence: The staging server, AKA the test environment, is being updated.

words.meaning: A prototype hardware device built to test a concept's feasibility.
words.sentence: We built a PoC device to demonstrate the sensor's accuracy.

words.meaning: In Real Life - referring to something happening outside the digital world.
words.sentence: Let's discuss this IRL instead of over chat.

words.meaning: Ready-made software or hardware products available for purchase, not custom-built.
words.sentence: We used a COTS solution instead of building this from scratch.

words.meaning: The stage when a product is fully released to all customers.
words.sentence: The feature reached GA after a few weeks in beta.

words.meaning: A pre-release version of software made available for testing.
words.sentence: We're testing the beta version before the public launch.

words.meaning: An early, often incomplete version of software used for internal testing.
words.sentence: The alpha version has several known bugs we're still fixing.

words.meaning: An older system still in use despite being outdated.
words.sentence: We're finally replacing the legacy system after ten years.

words.meaning: The person who ultimately uses a product or service.
words.sentence: We always keep the end user's needs in mind when designing features.

words.meaning: The process of introducing a new user, employee, or customer to a system.
words.sentence: Our onboarding flow walks new users through the key features.

words.meaning: The rate at which customers stop using a product or service.
words.sentence: We reduced churn by improving our customer support response times.

words.meaning: The point when a product or version stops being supported or sold.
words.sentence: This software version reached EOL, so we need to upgrade.

quiz.question: What does KPI stand for?
quiz.explanation: KPIs are measurable values tracking progress toward goals.

quiz.question: What does SLA stand for?
quiz.explanation: An SLA defines the expected standard of service between parties.

quiz.question: What does MVP mean in product development?
quiz.explanation: An MVP is a basic version of a product used to test ideas with real users.

quiz.question: ___ means 'as soon as possible.' (common abbreviation)
quiz.explanation: ASAP is used to request urgent action.

quiz.question: What is a 'legacy system'?
options: An older system still in use despite being outdated
A brand new system just launched
quiz.answer: An older system still in use despite being outdated
quiz.explanation: Legacy systems are outdated but still actively used.
